title: /lse
text: /lse Copy language setups to an XML file. /lse { /lan= Language } { /var= Library } FileName Switches /lan= /var= Description Copy the source for the language setup identified by Language and Library to File . The export format is XML. If the /lan sub-switch is omitted, all the languages in library are copied. If the /var sub-switch is omitted, the versions of language from all libraries are copied to file. If both the /lan and the /var sub-switches are omitted, all language setups are copied. Remember that when you copy these definitions from File into a Repository, you must compile the language setups before they can be used. Use the command line switch /mes or choose File >Compile from one of the language setup editors. Example: Using /lse The following example copies all language setups from the library MYLIB to the file lang.xml : /lse /var=MYLIB /lan=USA lang.xml? Copy Related Links /mes Compile global messages, help texts, and language setups. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
